# Transcrição: 2. No Defined Follow-Up Process

- **Fonte:** 2. No Defined Follow-Up Process
- **Duração:** 02:22
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:22]

The number two biggest mistake with internet leads is no defined follow-up process. First you didn't respond quick enough or at all, then you didn't respond quick enough and now it's like well what's my process here? Do you really think you're gonna just call them back and all of a sudden they're gonna be like yeah let's do it. Particularly if it was 44 hours ago or six hours ago. I mean you they don't even know they hit your site because you got to understand the night they hit your site they also hit the local grocery store site. You know I can get my groceries online now I can go online see what I want go tomorrow and pick them up. I could even have them delivered across the street to me so I could have hit in any one given time a car dealership I could have searched for a house I could have searched for selling a house in another city buying a house in one buying a car in the new and selling a car in the old one my groceries in the new and the restaurant I'm gonna go to Saturday night and who knows how many other sites I hit or how much time I spent on my tablet while I was doing that playing Candy Crush. And the fact that Candy Crush won't be here by the time you watch this just shows you this entire space is morphing and you need to be on your toes okay. I could be going from hip-hop to praying all in the same 30-minute period of time. Look there's random events going on for this person so what am I telling you and why am I telling you this you need a process you need a process that's gonna weigh out all these things and activities. When I respond the first time how's that different than the second one what do I do in the first 30 minutes the first hour the first two hours the first three hours is that too much is there a four hour response a five hour what do I do in the first day that's 24 hours just the first 24 hours and how could I do something every hour in the first 24 hours that would have variety and that wouldn't make people feel like man this guy won't leave me alone could you be creative enough to actually create a follow-up process for 24 hours because we have we've created a process not just for 24 hours but the entire 12 months consider the average person will not even respond to two twice to a lead that hit their website go to Macy's oh go to Sears go to JC Penny go to any retail store in the world in fact don't go to their store hit their website you'll never hear back from them again
